steps.title: npm account compromise and malicious package injection
steps.text: Attackers hijacked an Axios maintainer's npm account and released compromised versions (v1.14.1 and v0.30.4) that injected plain-crypto-js as a hidden dependency without modifying Axios source code
steps.description: Attackers hijacked an Axios maintainer's npm account and released compromised versions (v1.14.1 and v0.30.4) that injected plain-crypto-js as a hidden dependency without modifying Axios source code

steps.title: Postinstall dropper execution via npm lifecycle hook
steps.text: When developers run npm install axios, the malicious plain-crypto-js dependency triggers npm's postinstall lifecycle hook, executing a heavily obfuscated Node.js dropper script (setup.js) using two-layer encoding with string reversal, Base64-decoding and XOR cipher
steps.description: When developers run npm install axios, the malicious plain-crypto-js dependency triggers npm's postinstall lifecycle hook, executing a heavily obfuscated Node.js dropper script (setup.js) using two-layer encoding with string reversal, Base64-decoding and XOR cipher

steps.title: C2 communication with platform detection
steps.text: The dropper queries the operating system and sends HTTP POST requests to C2 server sfrclak[.]com:8000, disguising traffic as benign npm registry requests with platform-specific paths (packages.npm[.]org/product0/1/2)
steps.description: The dropper queries the operating system and sends HTTP POST requests to C2 server sfrclak[.]com:8000, disguising traffic as benign npm registry requests with platform-specific paths (packages.npm[.]org/product0/1/2)
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for T1082, T1071.001, T1105 indicators

steps.title: Platform-specific RAT payload deployment
steps.text: C2 server delivers different payloads per OS: macOS receives C++ Mach-O binary saved to /Library/Caches/com.apple.act.mond, Windows gets PowerShell RAT disguised as Windows Terminal (wt.exe), Linux receives Python RAT script (/tmp/ld.py)
steps.description: C2 server delivers different payloads per OS: macOS receives C++ Mach-O binary saved to /Library/Caches/com.apple.act.mond, Windows gets PowerShell RAT disguised as Windows Terminal (wt.exe), Linux receives Python RAT script (/tmp/ld.py)

steps.title: Cross-platform RAT execution and persistence
steps.text: All three RAT variants establish persistence using platform-specific methods: macOS via silent execution, Windows via registry Run keys and disguised PowerShell binary, Linux via nohup background execution. All use identical C2 protocol with 60-second beacons
steps.description: All three RAT variants establish persistence using platform-specific methods: macOS via silent execution, Windows via registry Run keys and disguised PowerShell binary, Linux via nohup background execution. All use identical C2 protocol with 60-second beacons
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for T1547.001, T1036.005, T1059.001, T1059.006 indicators

steps.title: Remote access and command execution capabilities
steps.text: The unified RAT framework accepts four commands: kill (self-terminate), runscript (execute shell/script commands), peinject (drop and execute binary payloads), and rundir (enumerate directories). All variants use anachronistic IE8/WinXP user-agent for evasion
steps.description: The unified RAT framework accepts four commands: kill (self-terminate), runscript (execute shell/script commands), peinject (drop and execute binary payloads), and rundir (enumerate directories). All variants use anachronistic IE8/WinXP user-agent for evasion
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for T1219, T1059, T1083, T1105 indicators

steps.title: Anti-forensic cleanup and evidence destruction
steps.text: After successful payload deployment (15 seconds total), the Node.js dropper performs aggressive cleanup by deleting setup.js, removing postinstall hooks, and replacing tampered package.json with clean decoy file (package.md)
steps.description: After successful payload deployment (15 seconds total), the Node.js dropper performs aggressive cleanup by deleting setup.js, removing postinstall hooks, and replacing tampered package.json with clean decoy file (package.md)
